disability.summary: Decoding and reading fluency are significantly below grade level. Listening comprehension is at or above grade level. With text-to-speech, Marcus performs grade-appropriate analytical work; without it, his comprehension collapses around dense or multi-clause sentences.

this_year.ela: I want to read longer texts without my eyes getting tired.
this_year.math: I want to do word problems faster.
this_year.science: I want to write up my own lab reports.

long_term: Get into the school robotics elective.

academics_general.current_performance: Strong oral participation; struggles with dense print. Listening comprehension at G7. Reading rate ~75 wpm (G7 target ~150). Spelling errors are phonetic and frequent.
academics_general.strengths: Quick verbal reasoner. Asks sharp clarifying questions. Strong with diagrams and physical models.
academics_general.impact: Reading fluency bottleneck slows him on every print-heavy task. Without accommodations he runs out of time before he can demonstrate what he knows.

ela.current_performance: Decoding accuracy ~88% on G7 text. Comprehension drops sharply on multi-clause sentences. With text-to-speech, comprehension ~grade level on the same text. Writing: ideas are strong; spelling and mechanics are well below grade level.
ela.strengths: When text is read aloud or via TTS, he engages thoughtfully and contributes original analysis.

math.current_performance: On-grade conceptually. Word problems gated by reading speed — give him the same problem read aloud and accuracy jumps from ~55% to ~85%.

behavioral_social_emotional.current_performance: Generally engaged and well-liked. Becomes self-deprecating about reading in front of peers; will refuse to read aloud. Responds well to choice (which scaffold to use) and to being treated as a capable thinker.
behavioral_social_emotional.impact: Avoidance of any task that requires reading aloud unprompted. Loses confidence quickly when called on cold.

response: Oral responses accepted in place of writing for comprehension checks
timing_scheduling: Frequent breaks on long reading blocks
setting_environment: Headphones permitted for TTS without singling out
Seated near a charging outlet for assistive device

modifications.instruction: Provide all texts in TTS-compatible format. Pre-teach key vocabulary. Never require unprompted oral reading.

annual_goals.baseline: Reading rate ~75 wpm on G7 text; decoding accuracy ~88%.
annual_goals.goal: Increase reading rate to 110 wpm on G7 text with 95% decoding accuracy.
benchmarks: Decode multi-syllable words using taught morphology rules.
Track text without finger or marker for 1 minute.
Re-read for fluency after first cold read.

annual_goals.baseline: With speech-to-text, produces on-grade ideas; mechanics still below grade level.
annual_goals.goal: Produce a 5-sentence on-grade analytical paragraph using speech-to-text + spell check, with 4 of 5 sentences free of meaning-altering errors.
annual_goals.method: Work samples scored against grade-level rubric
benchmarks: Dictate a complete claim sentence answering the prompt.
Dictate two pieces of supporting evidence with paragraph references.
Edit dictated text for missing punctuation and homophone errors.

annual_goals.baseline: Will use TTS when set up by an adult; rarely requests it himself.
annual_goals.goal: Independently request and set up his accommodations (TTS, headphones, extra time) in 4 of 5 opportunities.
benchmarks: Set up TTS on a new device unprompted.
Politely decline unprompted oral reading without disengaging.

placement: Full Inclusion Program (services outside gen ed < 21% of time)

mcas: Text-to-speech for ELA reading passages and questions
